amendments.change: ADR-0031 Amendment 1 (architect-reviewer APPROVE 2026-04-30) reshaped Job aggregate from flat command/args fields to nested driver: WorkloadDriver tagged enum with WorkloadDriver::Exec(Exec { command, args }) as the only Phase-1 variant. AllocationSpec stays flat per ADR-0030 §6 (which explicitly predicted per-driver-class spec types as the Phase 2+ shape). Validation field name 'exec.command' STAYS — operator-facing path matches TOML, internal Rust nesting does not leak. Action shim unchanged. Steps 01-01, 02-01, 05-01 ACs revised in place to reference the WorkloadDriver shape. Steps 01-02, 04-02 unchanged (validation field name stays; OpenAPI surface stays — WorkloadDriver/Exec are intent-shape and explicitly do NOT derive utoipa::ToSchema).

phases.name: Slice 1 — JobSpecInput + Job aggregate carry exec block; validation rule lands at Job::from_spec

steps.name: Slice 1A — reshape JobSpecInput (nested [resources] + [exec] tables, tagged DriverInput) and grow Job aggregate with command + args rkyv-archived fields
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from ADR-0031 §2 (JobSpecInput Rust shape) + §3 (Job aggregate extension); test seeds in distill/test-scenarios.md (Round-trip and Happy-path scenarios); production-code targets in distill/wave-decisions.md DWD-1 through DWD-3:
- crates/overdrive-core/src/aggregate/mod.rs: JobSpecInput is reshaped from flat {id, replicas, cpu_milli, memory_bytes} to {id, replicas, resources: ResourcesInput, #[serde(flatten)] driver: DriverInput} with #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]
- New nested types live in the same module: ResourcesInput {cpu_milli, memory_bytes}, ExecInput {command: String, args: Vec<String>}, DriverInput as a #[serde(rename_all = "snake_case", deny_unknown_fields)] tagged enum with one variant Exec(ExecInput) — implicit-by-table-name dispatch (TOML [exec] table → DriverInput::Exec)
- All four input types derive Serialize, Deserialize, utoipa::ToSchema (per ADR-0031 §8; extended in step 04-02). Each carries #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]
- Job aggregate (intent shape) grows ONE field: driver: WorkloadDriver per ADR-0031 Amendment 1 (NOT flat command/args — that was the original §3 shape, now SUPERSEDED). New types in same module: WorkloadDriver enum with one variant Exec(Exec); Exec struct {command: String, args: Vec<String>}. Both carry the same derive set as Job: Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize, rkyv::Archive, rkyv::Serialize, rkyv::Deserialize. The rkyv archive byte shape changes — single-cut hash break is intentional per ADR-0031 §9 / DWD-9. WorkloadDriver and Exec do NOT derive utoipa::ToSchema (intent shape, not wire shape — see step 04-02)
- From<&Job> for JobSpecInput updated: destructure let WorkloadDriver::Exec(exec) = &job.driver and project to wire shape (resources: ResourcesInput { cpu_milli: job.resources.cpu_milli, memory_bytes: job.resources.memory_bytes }, driver: DriverInput::Exec(ExecInput { command: exec.command.clone(), args: exec.args.clone() }))
- Job::from_spec destructures the wire shape AND projects to the intent shape: let JobSpecInput { id, replicas, resources: ResourcesInput { cpu_milli, memory_bytes }, driver: DriverInput::Exec(ExecInput { command, args }) } = spec; ... ; Job { ..., driver: WorkloadDriver::Exec(Exec { command, args }) }. Validation logic for command stays as todo!("RED scaffold: ADR-0031 §4 ...") — that lands in 01-02; this step only widens the shape

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md):
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_constructors.rs::job_from_spec_preserves_operator_command_casing_verbatim — Job.command is preserved byte-for-byte (no normalization)
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_constructors.rs::job_from_spec_accepts_non_empty_command_with_empty_args_vec — args = [] is valid (per ADR-0031 §4)
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_constructors.rs::job_from_spec_accepts_empty_string_and_whitespace_in_args_vec — argv elements are opaque (no per-element rule)
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_roundtrip.rs::jobspec_input_roundtrips_through_aggregate_with_exec_block — JobSpecInput::from(&Job::from_spec(input.clone()).unwrap()) == input
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_roundtrip.rs::jobspec_input_roundtrips_with_empty_args_vec — round-trip identity with args = []
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_roundtrip.rs::jobspec_input_roundtrip_property_with_exec_block — proptest 1024 cases
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/aggregate_roundtrip.rs::job_rkyv_byte_identical_on_repeated_archival — existing test, sample fixture migrates to new shape (covered in 05-02 fixture sweep)
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/aggregate_roundtrip.rs::job_serde_json_roundtrip_equals_original — existing test, sample fixture migrates to new shape (covered in 05-02)

Quality gates:
- cargo check -p overdrive-core green
- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --no-run for the 3 new acceptance files compiles cleanly (the BROKEN pre-existing fixture files in DWD-16 stay BROKEN until 05-02; that's the documented single-cut posture per .claude/rules/testing.md § "RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits")
- cargo run -p xtask -- dst-lint exits 0 (overdrive-core stays class core; new BTreeMap discipline applies)

Reuse Analysis cross-ref (from design/wave-decisions.md): three CREATE NEW types (ResourcesInput, ExecInput, DriverInput) — each justified at design time, not relitigated here.
steps.description: Slice 1A of the wire-up. Pure type-shape work: introduces ResourcesInput / ExecInput / DriverInput tagged-enum nesting on JobSpecInput AND mirrors the tagged-enum on the intent side as Job.driver: WorkloadDriver per ADR-0031 Amendment 1 (NOT flat command/args on Job — that was the original §3 shape, now SUPERSEDED). Validation logic (the trimmed-non-empty rule for exec.command) is deliberately deferred to 01-02 to keep this step's diff scoped to type changes; the existing todo!("RED scaffold: ADR-0031 §4 ...") at aggregate/mod.rs:143-144 stays in place until 01-02 replaces it. The pre-existing fixture-shape mismatches enumerated in DWD-16 stay BROKEN through this step — they migrate atomically with the production code in 05-02 per the single-cut migration discipline (DWD-9). Independent of every other slice; depends on nothing.

steps.name: Slice 1B — Job::from_spec validation rule for exec.command (trimmed-non-empty rejection per ADR-0031 §4)
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from ADR-0031 §4 (validation rules) + design/upstream-changes.md (validation rule list); production-code target in DWD-4:
- crates/overdrive-core/src/aggregate/mod.rs:143-144: replace the todo!("RED scaffold: ADR-0031 §4 — Job::from_spec must reject empty / whitespace exec.command") with the real validation: if command.trim().is_empty() return Err(AggregateError::Validation { field: "exec.command", message: "command must be non-empty".to_string() })
- The structured AggregateError::Validation { field, message } shape is mandatory — string-only assertions are rejected per .claude/rules/development.md § "Errors" and DWD-5
- The field name is exactly "exec.command" (matches design/upstream-changes.md). Whitespace-only inputs ("   ", "\t\n\r", mixed Unicode whitespace) all reject because trim() handles all Unicode whitespace classes
- No NUL-byte rejection (kernel execve(2) handles); no length cap (kernel PATH_MAX handles); no per-element args rule (per ADR-0031 §4)
- command casing preserved verbatim — the validator is a predicate, not a normalizer (see 01-01's casing-preservation test which becomes a proper GREEN test once this step lands)

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md):
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_validation.rs::job_from_spec_rejects_empty_exec_command_with_structured_field_name — empty string → AggregateError::Validation { field: "exec.command", .. }
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_validation.rs::job_from_spec_rejects_whitespace_only_exec_command_via_trim_rule — "   " → same shape
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_validation.rs::job_from_spec_rejects_mixed_whitespace_exec_command — mixed Unicode whitespace → same shape
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_validation.rs::empty_or_whitespace_command_always_yields_exec_command_validation — proptest 1024 cases over generated whitespace strings
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_validation.rs::toml_missing_exec_table_fails_to_parse_with_serde_error — schema-level rejection at deserialise time (no [exec] table → serde error before from_spec runs)
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_validation.rs::toml_missing_resources_table_fails_to_parse_with_serde_error — schema-level rejection (no [resources] table)
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_validation.rs::toml_with_unknown_top_level_driver_table_fails_to_parse_via_deny_unknown_fields — deny_unknown_fields catches a hypothetical [microvm] / duplicate [exec] / typo'd table
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_validation.rs::toml_with_typo_in_exec_field_fails_via_deny_unknown_fields — deny_unknown_fields on ExecInput catches a typo'd field name

Quality gates:
- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --no-run for exec_validation.rs compiles cleanly
- All 8 exec_validation.rs tests transition from RED (panic on todo!()) to GREEN
- cargo run -p xtask -- dst-lint exits 0

Reconciler purity (system-wide constraint per ADR-0013): from_spec is a sync constructor — no .await, no Instant::now, no IO. The validation predicate is pure over its input.
steps.description: Slice 1B of the wire-up. Replaces the todo!("RED scaffold: ADR-0031 §4 ...") at crates/overdrive-core/src/aggregate/mod.rs:143-144 with the trimmed-non-empty validation rule for exec.command. After GREEN, all 8 exec_validation.rs tests pass; the structured AggregateError::Validation { field: "exec.command", .. } variant is asserted by every test (no string-contains assertions per DWD-5). Schema-level rejections (deny_unknown_fields, missing tables) are also covered — those fire at serde deserialise time, before from_spec sees the value. Depends on 01-01 (the new fields must exist before validation can reference them).

phases.name: Slice 2 — Action variants carry validated spec; reconciler projects from Job (depends on Slice 1)

steps.name: Slice 2 — Action::StartAllocation/RestartAllocation grow spec: AllocationSpec; JobLifecycle::reconcile destructures &job.driver as WorkloadDriver::Exec(Exec { command, args }) and projects to flat AllocationSpec
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from ADR-0031 §5 (Action enum revision) + ADR-0013 (reconciler primitive runtime); production-code target in DWD-6:
- crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs (or wherever Action lives): Action::StartAllocation grows a spec: AllocationSpec field; Action::RestartAllocation grows from {alloc_id} to {alloc_id, spec: AllocationSpec}
- AllocationSpec is the existing type from crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/driver.rs (already carries flat command + args per ADR-0030 — confirmed at lines 108-121; stays flat per ADR-0031 Amendment 1 + ADR-0030 §6 which predicts per-driver-class spec types as the Phase 2+ shape)
- JobLifecycle::reconcile body is a sync pure projection. For each Start/Restart action emitted, destructure let WorkloadDriver::Exec(exec) = &job.driver (irrefutable in Phase 1 single-variant; Phase 2+ adds variants and the destructure becomes a match), then build the AllocationSpec inline as: AllocationSpec { alloc, identity, command: exec.command.clone(), args: exec.args.clone(), resources: job.resources.clone() }
- Reconciler purity preserved: NO .await, NO Instant::now, NO SystemTime::now, NO direct store writes inside reconcile. The two .clone() calls are pure deterministic projections per ADR-0031 §Compliance lines 556-560
- ADR-0013 twin-invocation invariant continues to hold: two reconcile() calls with bit-identical (desired, actual, view, tick) inputs produce bit-identical (Vec<Action>, NextView) outputs
- xtask dst-lint passes (overdrive-core stays class core; the reconciler body must remain banned-API-clean)

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md):
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_reconciler_purity.rs::start_action_carries_full_alloc_spec_from_live_job_command_and_args — given a Job with driver: WorkloadDriver::Exec(Exec { command: "/bin/echo", args: ["hi"] }), reconcile produces Action::StartAllocation { spec: AllocationSpec { command: "/bin/echo", args: ["hi"], .. }, .. } — the projection destructures Job.driver and populates the flat AllocationSpec
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_reconciler_purity.rs::restart_action_carries_full_alloc_spec_from_live_job — same property for Action::RestartAllocation; spec.command + spec.args + spec.resources are all populated by destructuring &job.driver as WorkloadDriver::Exec(Exec { command, args })
- crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/exec_reconciler_purity.rs::reconcile_with_exec_spec_is_deterministic_across_twin_invocations — calls reconcile() twice with bit-identical inputs and asserts byte-equal (Vec<Action>, NextView) outputs (the ADR-0013 purity invariant)

Quality gates:
- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --no-run for exec_reconciler_purity.rs compiles cleanly
- All 3 exec_reconciler_purity.rs tests transition from RED to GREEN
- cargo run -p xtask -- dst-lint exits 0 (banned-API check on JobLifecycle::reconcile body)
- ReconcilerIsPure invariant in crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators/ continues to pass with the new spec field on the Action variants (the harness re-types AnyState match arms to construct the new shape; that wiring is mechanical)

Deleted-from-shim helpers (Phase 03 deletes them; Phase 02's role is to make the action carry enough state that the deletion is safe): build_phase1_restart_spec, build_identity, default_restart_resources. Once the spec is on the action, the shim can read it straight off without rebuilding from observation rows.
steps.description: Slice 2 of the wire-up. Grows Action::StartAllocation and Action::RestartAllocation to carry spec: AllocationSpec (AllocationSpec stays flat per ADR-0030 + Amendment 1). Updates JobLifecycle::reconcile to destructure &job.driver as WorkloadDriver::Exec(Exec { command, args }) and project to the flat AllocationSpec command/args fields (which exist on AllocationSpec since ADR-0030; Job carries them nested under .driver since Amendment 1, landed in 01-01). Pure deterministic projection — no .await, no IO, no wall-clock reads inside reconcile per ADR-0013. The twin-invocation purity invariant (asserting byte-equal output across two identical-input calls) is the load-bearing test. After GREEN, the action variants carry everything the action shim needs to dispatch — Phase 03 then deletes the hardcoded /bin/sleep 60 fabrication in build_phase1_restart_spec. Depends on Slice 1 (Job aggregate must already carry command + args).

phases.name: Slice 3 — Action shim deletion: dispatcher reads spec straight off the action (depends on Slice 2)

steps.name: Slice 3 — delete build_phase1_restart_spec / build_identity / default_restart_resources from action_shim.rs; rewrite Restart match arm to read spec from action
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from ADR-0031 §6 (action shim deletions) + ADR-0023 (action shim placement); production-code target in DWD-7:
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim.rs: physically delete the functions build_phase1_restart_spec (lines 220-231 in the pre-deletion file), build_identity, default_restart_resources, and the test default_restart_resources_pins_exact_values
- The Restart match arm in the dispatcher reads spec straight off the action. Replace the prior pattern ("build a spec from helpers") with: let AllocationSpec { alloc, identity, command, args, resources } = spec.clone() (or pass spec by reference directly to driver.start)
- Stateless dispatcher contract preserved per ADR-0023: the shim signature is &dyn Driver + &dyn ObservationStore (no IntentStore handle, no rebuild-from-observation logic)
- The deletion is permanent. Do not leave compatibility shims, deprecation comments, or // removed in PR markers per CLAUDE.md and feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md
- The trybuild compile-fail proof for the deletion was deferred per DWD-7 (rustc-version-sensitive .stderr golden + net-new trybuild infrastructure on overdrive-control-plane). The behavioural test below is the load-bearing substitute: it fails if any future maintainer reintroduces the fabrication into the dispatch path

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md):
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/action_shim_restart_uses_spec_from_action.rs::action_shim_restart_passes_spec_from_action_to_driver_start_unchanged — uses a RecordingDriver fake to capture every Driver::start(spec) invocation; asserts spec.command equals the action's command (not the deleted "/bin/sleep" literal), spec.args equals the action's args (not ["60"]), spec.resources equals the action's resources (not the default_restart_resources fabrication)

Quality gates:
- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --no-run for action_shim_restart_uses_spec_from_action.rs compiles cleanly
- The behavioural test passes
- cargo clippy -p overdrive-control-plane --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings exits 0 (any unused-import / dead-code warning from the deleted helpers must be cleaned up in the same change)
- grep -rn "build_phase1_restart_spec\|build_identity\|default_restart_resources" crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/ returns zero matches (the deletions are real, not just dead code)

Deletion discipline (per CLAUDE.md and feedback_delete_dont_gate.md): the deleted test default_restart_resources_pins_exact_values goes away with the function it defended — no salvage, no rewrite. The behavioural test action_shim_restart_passes_spec_from_action_to_driver_start_unchanged is a NEW test for the new contract, not a relabeled version of the old one.
steps.description: Slice 3 of the wire-up. Deletes the three helper functions in action_shim.rs (build_phase1_restart_spec, build_identity, default_restart_resources) plus the test that defended default_restart_resources. Rewrites the Restart match arm in the dispatcher to read spec straight off the action — possible because Slice 2 grew Action::RestartAllocation to carry the validated spec. The trybuild compile-fail proof was deferred per DWD-7; the behavioural RecordingDriver test is the substitute. After GREEN, the dispatcher is a pure stateless projection from action variants to driver calls; the prior "shim fabricates state from observation" pattern is permanently gone. Depends on Slice 2 (Action::RestartAllocation must carry spec before the dispatcher can read it).

phases.name: Slice 4 — API surface: server-side handler defence-in-depth + OpenAPI snapshot regeneration (depends on Slice 3)

steps.name: Slice 4A — submit_job server handler runs Job::from_spec (defence-in-depth per ADR-0015); empty/whitespace exec.command → 400 with structured ControlPlaneError::Validation
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from ADR-0031 §7 (validating-constructor lane preservation) + ADR-0015 (HTTP error mapping); production-code target in DWD-14:
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/handlers.rs: the submit_job handler already routes through Job::from_spec per ADR-0011. With 01-02's validation rule landed, the same handler now rejects empty/whitespace exec.command at the server boundary (defence-in-depth — the CLI also validates client-side per ADR-0015, but server validates regardless)
- The validation error maps to ControlPlaneError::Validation { field: Some("exec.command"), message } per the existing error-mapping at handlers.rs (look for the existing AggregateError → ControlPlaneError mapping and verify it preserves the field name unchanged)
- HTTP response: 400 Bad Request with structured JSON body { "error": "validation", "field": "exec.command", "message": "command must be non-empty" } per ADR-0015 wire shape
- No new error variant on ControlPlaneError — the existing Validation variant carries field + message

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md):
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/submit_job_handler_rejects_empty_exec_command_with_400.rs::submit_job_handler_rejects_empty_exec_command_with_validation_error_naming_field — POST JobSpecInput with empty exec.command → asserts ControlPlaneError::Validation { field: Some("exec.command"), .. } returned
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/submit_job_handler_rejects_empty_exec_command_with_400.rs::submit_job_handler_rejects_whitespace_only_exec_command_with_validation_error — same property for whitespace-only command

The tests run against the in-process axum harness (no real network, no subprocess) per the project's testing.md acceptance-vs-integration discipline (DWD-10).

Quality gates:
- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --no-run for submit_job_handler_rejects_empty_exec_command_with_400.rs compiles cleanly
- Both tests transition from RED to GREEN
- cargo run -p xtask -- openapi-check exits 0 (the OpenAPI schema for the error response stays consistent with the structured JSON body)

Reconciler purity preserved: handler is async and may .await on the IntentStore put — that's fine; ADR-0013's purity contract applies to reconcile(), not to handlers.
steps.description: Slice 4A of the wire-up. Server-side defence-in-depth: the submit_job handler already routes through Job::from_spec, so once Slice 1B's validation rule lands the handler rejects empty/whitespace exec.command at the HTTP boundary too. The CLI validates client-side BEFORE any HTTP call (covered in 05-01); the server validates server-side for defence-in-depth per ADR-0015. After GREEN, the structured ControlPlaneError::Validation { field: Some(\"exec.command\"), .. } variant is asserted by both rejection scenarios. Depends on Slice 3 (the full path through to ExecDriver must be wired before the handler-level test reflects end-to-end behaviour).

steps.name: Slice 4B — utoipa::ToSchema on JobSpecInput / ResourcesInput / ExecInput / DriverInput; regenerate api/openapi.yaml golden via cargo xtask openapi-gen
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from ADR-0031 §8 (OpenAPI propagation) + ADR-0009 (OpenAPI schema derivation); production-code target in DWD-15:
- crates/overdrive-core/src/aggregate/mod.rs: utoipa::ToSchema derive on JobSpecInput stays (already present); add the same derive on ResourcesInput, ExecInput, DriverInput. Per ADR-0031 Amendment 1, the intent-shape types WorkloadDriver and Exec do NOT derive utoipa::ToSchema (they are not part of the API surface; the OpenAPI schema is derived from wire-shape types only — preserves state-layer hygiene)
- DriverInput's tagged-enum nature: utoipa renders the implicit-by-table-name shape as a oneOf or inline tagged variant per its tagged-enum support; the ADR ratifies this is acceptable for the single-driver case (additional driver variants in Phase 2+ extend the oneOf)
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/api.rs: the OverdriveApi macro registration list grows to include ResourcesInput, ExecInput, DriverInput so they appear in the components/schemas section of the generated YAML
- Run cargo xtask openapi-gen to regenerate api/openapi.yaml; commit the regenerated YAML as the new golden
- The old top-level cpu_milli + memory_bytes fields are gone from the JobSpecInput schema (replaced by the resources object); deny_unknown_fields renders correctly

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md):
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/openapi_exec_block.rs::openapi_schema_carries_jobspec_input_with_nested_resources_and_tagged_driver_exec_variant — generates the YAML in-process via OverdriveApi::openapi() and asserts the resources object + tagged-driver enum are present with command + args carried on the exec variant
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/openapi_exec_block.rs::openapi_schema_drops_flat_cpu_milli_field_from_jobspec_input_top_level — negative test: asserts the old flat cpu_milli field is NOT present at the JobSpecInput top level (the migration is real)
- crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/api_type_shapes.rs::every_api_type_implements_utoipa_to_schema — existing test extended to cover the 4 new types (ResourcesInput, ExecInput, DriverInput, plus reconfirms JobSpecInput)

Quality gates:
- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --no-run for openapi_exec_block.rs compiles cleanly
- Both openapi_exec_block tests transition from RED to GREEN
- cargo xtask openapi-check exits 0 (the regenerated api/openapi.yaml is byte-identical to the in-process renderer's output — the canonical drift detector)
- api/openapi.yaml is committed in the same change as the production code that drives the regen

Documentation (back-prop): no docs change needed — ADR-0009 already documents the regen pattern; ADR-0031 §8 already documents this feature's usage of it.
steps.description: Slice 4B of the wire-up. Adds utoipa::ToSchema derives to ResourcesInput / ExecInput / DriverInput; registers them in the OverdriveApi macro list; regenerates api/openapi.yaml via cargo xtask openapi-gen and commits the new golden. The two openapi_exec_block tests assert positive presence of the new shape AND negative absence of the old flat cpu_milli field — the migration is verified in both directions. The existing every_api_type_implements_utoipa_to_schema test in api_type_shapes.rs is extended to cover the 4 new types. Independent of 04-01 in principle (different files, different tests) but shares the dependency on Slice 3 (full wiring must exist).

phases.name: Slice 5 — CLI walking skeleton (with persistence assertion fix from DISTILL review) + single-cut fixture migration sweep (DWD-16)

steps.name: Slice 5A — CLI walking-skeleton end-to-end (incl. back-door IntentStore read per DISTILL review BLOCK fix); CLI parse-error path tests
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from distill/walking-skeleton.md + distill/review-from-acceptance-designer-reviewer.md (BLOCK fix); production-code target in DWD-8:
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/exec_spec_walking_skeleton.rs: the WS test already exists but currently only asserts spec_digest. Per the DISTILL review's single Critical issue, this step's AC adds a back-door IntentStore read at the canonical key jobs/payments and asserts: (a) the rkyv-archived bytes deserialise to a Job, (b) destructure let WorkloadDriver::Exec(exec) = &job.driver and assert exec.command == "/opt/payments/bin/payments-server" (operator's TOML input verbatim), (c) exec.args == vec!["--port".to_string(), "8080".to_string()] (operator's TOML input verbatim) — per ADR-0031 Amendment 1 the Job carries driver: WorkloadDriver, NOT flat command/args
- The back-door read pattern matches crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/submit_job_idempotency.rs (the canonical reference for IntentStore back-door reads in this project — see that file for the exact code shape)
- The WS test continues to call overdrive_cli::commands::job::submit(SubmitArgs { spec, config_path }) as a Rust function — NOT as a subprocess. Per crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md the project rejects subprocess CLI tests; the deviation is documented and load-bearing for DST discipline
- The WS test uses tempfile::TempDir for both the operator TOML file and the IntentStore (LocalStore over redb) — Strategy C (Real local) per distill/walking-skeleton.md
- CLI parse-error tests appended to crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/job_submit.rs surface CLI-side validation BEFORE any HTTP call (the operator gets the field-named error without a server round-trip per ADR-0011)

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md):
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/exec_spec_walking_skeleton.rs::walking_skeleton_submit_with_exec_block_returns_inserted_and_persists_command_and_args — happy path: submit, assert IdempotencyOutcome::Inserted + 64-char hex spec_digest + back-door IntentStore read confirms Job.command and Job.args persisted (the Critical-issue fix)
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/job_submit.rs::cli_submit_surfaces_missing_exec_table_as_toml_field_error — TOML missing the [exec] table → CliError::InvalidSpec { field: "toml", message } (the toml deserialise error surfaces under field="toml" per the existing CLI parse-error mapping)
- crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/job_submit.rs::cli_submit_rejects_empty_exec_command_before_any_http_call — empty exec.command → CliError::InvalidSpec { field: "exec.command", message } AND the test points the trust-triple endpoint at an unreachable address (127.0.0.1:1) to prove the rejection happens BEFORE any HTTP call (no Transport error reached)

Quality gates:
- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-cli --features integration-tests --no-run compiles cleanly for the touched test files
- All 3 tests above transition from RED to GREEN
- The back-door IntentStore read in the WS test is real — it actually calls store.get(IntentKey::for_job(&job_id).as_bytes()), deserialises the rkyv bytes, and asserts on the Job fields. NOT a stand-in / mock

The DISTILL review verdict was BLOCK on this single issue. After this step lands, the WS proves end-to-end data flow (validation + computation + persistence), not just validation + computation.
steps.description: Slice 5A of the wire-up. Walking-skeleton end-to-end test through the CLI handler (called as a Rust function per crates/overdrive-cli/CLAUDE.md, not subprocess). The DISTILL acceptance-designer-reviewer issued a BLOCK verdict on this exact test: it currently asserts only the spec_digest, but test-scenarios.md §1 and walking-skeleton.md both promised it would also back-door read the IntentStore at jobs/payments and assert Job.command + Job.args match the operator's TOML input. This step's AC explicitly includes that back-door read fix alongside the original digest assertion. The two CLI parse-error tests in job_submit.rs are also wired here. Depends on Slice 3 (the full path from CLI through HTTP through reconciler through driver must be wired before the WS round-trip succeeds).

steps.name: Slice 5B — single-cut fixture migration sweep (DWD-16): migrate ~13 BROKEN test files from flat cpu_milli/memory_bytes shape to nested [resources]/[exec] shape; commit alongside production code per DWD-9
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from DWD-16 (the named BROKEN file list) + DWD-9 (single-cut migration discipline) + .claude/rules/testing.md § "RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits":
- Migrate every TestFixture / sample_jobspec / sample_job helper in the following files from the flat shape (cpu_milli + memory_bytes at top level, no exec block) to the nested shape (resources: ResourcesInput { cpu_milli, memory_bytes }, driver: DriverInput::Exec(ExecInput { command, args })):
  - crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/aggregate_constructors.rs
  - crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/aggregate_roundtrip.rs
  - crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/aggregate_validation.rs
  - crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/first_fit_place_branches.rs
  - crates/overdrive-core/tests/acceptance/job_lifecycle_reconcile_branches.rs
  - crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/http_client.rs
  - crates/overdrive-cli/tests/integration/walking_skeleton.rs
  - crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/api_type_shapes.rs
  - crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/job_lifecycle_backoff.rs
  - crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/job_stop_idempotent.rs
  - crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/job_stop_intent_key.rs
  - crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs
  - crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/submit_job_idempotency.rs
  - crates/overdrive-sim/tests/acceptance/reconciler_is_pure_with_job_lifecycle.rs
  - all of crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/*.rs (the integration suite carrying fixture-shape mismatches)
- Each fixture's migration is mechanical: pick a sensible default exec (e.g. command = "/bin/true", args = vec![]) where the test does not care about the exec value; pick the operator-realistic value where it does (e.g. the WS-shape "/opt/payments/bin/payments-server" + ["--port", "8080"])
- No #[serde(alias = "cpu_milli")] shims, no two-shape acceptance period, no "migrate one fixture per PR" gradualism — single-cut per DWD-9 and feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md
- Commit lands the production code (Slices 1-4 + 05-01) AND the fixture migration in a single commit. Commit with git commit --no-verify per the documented exception in .claude/rules/testing.md § "RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits" (the per-PR final mutation gate runs in CI; the commit message explicitly notes the bypass and the reason)

GREEN signal:
- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run compiles cleanly with ZERO pre-existing fixture errors. Every workspace member's test binary builds.
- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests passes (Linux; on macOS run via cargo xtask lima run -- per .claude/rules/testing.md)
- cargo test --doc --workspace passes (any doctests using the JobSpecInput shape get migrated too — search for cpu_milli in src/**/*.rs doc comments)
- cargo run -p xtask -- dst-lint exits 0
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings exits 0

The sweep is mostly mechanical (high count, low cognitive load per file). Expected total: ~14 files + integration directory contents. Use a consistent helper extraction pattern if duplicated fixture-construction lines exceed ~3 occurrences per file (the rule of three from CLAUDE.md).

Final mutation gate (per .claude/rules/testing.md): cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests must pass with kill rate >= 80%. This runs once before opening the PR, not per-step. Mention the kill-rate result in the PR description.
steps.description: Slice 5B of the wire-up. The single-cut fixture migration sweep that DWD-16 named. ~14 pre-existing test files (acceptance + integration) currently use the flat cpu_milli/memory_bytes shape and fail to compile against the new nested shape — that's the documented RED-by-design state per .claude/rules/testing.md. This step migrates every one of them in a single commit alongside the production code change (Slices 1-4 + 05-01). No deprecation aliases, no two-shape period — single-cut migration per DWD-9 and feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md. Commit with git commit --no-verify per the documented exception (the lefthook gate would block on the pre-migration RED state; CI's per-PR mutation gate is the actual quality bar). Depends on Slice 1 first step (the new JobSpecInput shape must exist before fixtures can be migrated to it); in practice ships in the same change-set as everything else.
steps.time_estimate: ~1-1.5 days (~14 files, mechanical per file but high count)
